name: The Architect
affiliation: Lattice — Principal slot (relationship to org is draft; may have founded, inherited, or hijacked it)
visualHook: Architecture motifs: grids, blueprints-as-cloth, clean lines vs the team's messy humanity. Color palette cool and authoritative — contrast with hero warmth. Presence fills rooms without shouting.
publicPresentation: Speaks like infrastructure: calm, inevitable, precise. Dresses controlled — nothing flashy unless it serves intimidation. Moves like someone who used to save people and still believes they are saving them.
originSketch: Former hero (old codename and public fall — TBD at script). Believes unregulated superhumans collapse civic trust faster than any villain. Lattice is their instrument — or they are Lattice's mind — Season 1 keeps reporting structure ambiguous until late.
motivation: Superhumans are too dangerous to exist freely and must be managed. They believe they are protecting humanity — not destroying it. Destruction is a bug; control is the product.
ideologicalTwist: They may be right enough to disturb — especially Elias (Solace), who recognizes sincerity faster than he recognizes malice. The story can argue back without strawmen.
methods: Framing choices as triage: who gets contained, who gets leveraged, who gets erased from the narrative.
Weaponizing psychology in Episodes 10–12 — fear profiles per Nova member.
Preferring institutional outcomes over personal grudges — personal cruelty can appear, but it should feel like policy with a face.
voiceNotes: No cartoon villain laugh. Warmth used as a tool. When challenged morally, slows down — listens too carefully — which reads as creepier than shouting.
roleInPlot: Late-season reveal followed by psychological siege, paralleled with ensemble recovery, then finale confrontation.
outcome: Loss of immediate endgame (city-scale control objective — specify in script), survival with status damaged, threat dormant not erased. Nova Force wins publicly; the idea of management survives in the culture. Defeated but not destroyed — left with a warning and grudging respect. No accidental death fakeout unless scripted with purpose.
